data.title: Advanced Micro Devices expects $800M charge due to new U.S. export controls
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) expects new licensing requirements to export its MI308 artificial intelligence chips to China may result in charges of up to $800M.

Shares of AMD fell 6% during early market trading on Wednesday.

"On April 15, 2025, Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. completed its initial assessment of a new license requirement implemented by the United States government for the export of certain semiconductor products to China (including Hong Kong and Macau) and D:5 countries, or to companies headquartered or with an ultimate parent in such countries," AMD said in a Form 8-K filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

"The Export Control applies to the Company’s MI308 products," the report added. "The Company expects to apply for licenses, but there is no assurance that licenses will be granted. The Company expects that the Export Control may result in charges of up to approximately $800 million in inventory, purchase commitments and related reserves."

This follows a similar hit to Nvidia's (NVDA) H20 graphic processing units, which resulted in a $5.5B charge.

data.title: Sell-rated S&P 500 stocks as per Seeking Alpha's quant model
data.description:
With the first quarter earnings season underway, energy (XLE), materials (XLB) and technology (XLK) emerged as the lowest-rated sectors among the 11 S&P 500 (SP500) sectors, as per Seeking Alpha's Quant model.

Seeking Alpha's Quant system consistently outperforms the S&P 500 (SP500), driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics.

Lowest-ranked sectors

Energy (XLE), Materials (XLB) and Technology (XLK) represented the lowest ranked sectors as per SA’s Quant model, with energy and materials being the only sectors among the 11 S&P 500 sectors with a Strong Sell rating.

Among S&P 500 energy stocks, Gladstone Land (LAND) got a Sell rating, while Halliburton (HAL), Phillips 66 (PSX), ONEOK (OKE), Chevron (CVX) and Kinder Morgan (KMI) were among stocks with a Hold rating.

In materials, Albemarle (ALB) received a Sell rating with a Quant score of 1.55.

Here are the Lowest-Rated S&P 500 Stocks Per SA Quant System:

  • Moderna (MRNA); SA Quant score of 1.19 (Strong Sell)
  • Charles River Laboratories (CRL); SA Quant score of 1.31 (Strong Sell)
  • Teradyne (TER); SA Quant score of 1.34 (Strong Sell)
  • Crown Castle (CCI); SA Quant score of 1.37 (Strong Sell)
  • West Pharmaceutical Services (WST); SA Quant score of 1.4 (Strong Sell)
  • Microchip Technology (MCHP); SA Quant score of 1.48 (Strong Sell)
  • SBA Communications (SBAC); SA Quant score of 1.54 (Sell)
  • Albemarle (ALB); SA Quant score of 1.55 (Sell)
  • Gladstone Land (LAND); SA Quant score of 1.73 (Sell)
  • Zebra Technologies (ZBRA); SA Quant score of 1.82 (Sell)

data.title: Industrial production dips more than expected in March
data.description: U.S. industrial production fell 0.3% M/M in March, weaker than the -0.2% consensus and +0.8% in February (revised from +0.7%), according to data released by the Federal Reserve on Wednesday.

data.description:

data.title: Biomerica stock plummets on 1-for-8 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Biomerica (NASDAQ:BMRA) has announced that it will implement a 1-for-8 reverse stock split of the issued and outstanding shares of its common stock, effective at 12:01 a.m. ET on April 21, 2025.
  • The Reverse Stock Split is intended to increase the bid price of the common stock to enable Biomerica to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement for continued listing on The Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • This will reduce the number of shares outstanding from ~20.4M shares to ~2.55M shares, and the number of authorized shares of Biomerica’s common stock will not change.
  • BMRA -29.9% premarket to $0.40.

data.title: Snowflake appears better built to handle macro volatility than last downturn: BTIG
data.description:
Snowflake (NYSE:SNOW) has modified some business practices, which makes the cloud data company better equipped to handle economic volatility, according to BTIG.

Ahead of Snowflake's first quarter fiscal 2026 financial results, BTIG reiterated its Buy rating and $220 price target on the stock. The financial services firm remains confident Snowflake will equal or surpass market estimates expecting 23% product revenue growth in fiscal 2026.

Snowflake has initiated proactive customer engagement practices in order to increase its net retention rate. Its largest customers also have healthier fundamentals and more stability.

During the last downturn, following the COVID-19 epidemic, Snowflake's surging product revenue slowed dramatically, from 106% in fiscal 2022 to 70% in fiscal 2023 and 38% in fiscal 2024.

"The slope of this deceleration was much greater than many expected," said BTIG analysts Gray Powell and Trevor Rambo, in a Wednesday investor note. "Again, this was related to a weakening economic environment and customer cost optimization initiatives. Over the last 12-18 months, SNOW has taken proactive measures to make sure existing customers are optimized and not overspending on the platform. If the team sees a spike in any customer's usage over a short period of time, there is a proactive outreach. In addition, customers today have better tools to monitor consumption and gain better visibility on workloads."

BTIG also identifies the potential for artificial intelligence workloads going into production by the middle of the year, which would increase consumption levels during the second half of fiscal 2026.

Snowflake is expected to release its first quarter fiscal 2026 financial results on May 22. A consensus estimate calls for adjusted earnings per share of $0.21 and revenue of $1.01B.

data.title: Nvidia H20 ban could have 'silver lining,' UBS suggests
data.description:
While many analysts believe that Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) needing an indefinite license to export — and essentially being blocked — to sell its H20 GPUs to China is a negative for the Jensen Huang-led company, UBS said it could come with a silver lining. 

Shares fell approximately 6% in premarket trading.

“While clearly reducing NVDA’s ability to beat and raise, this may be a clearing event for the stock if in exchange for committing to build $500B of AI infrastructure in the US over the next [4 years], NVDA used this as concession to effectively kill the AI Diffusion Rule,” analyst Timothy Arcuri wrote in a note to clients.

Earlier this week, a group of Republican senators called on the Trump administration to get rid of the AI Diffusion rule — which was designed to limit the spread of advanced AI technology outside the U.S. — on the grounds that it could impact the U.S.'s leadership in artificial intelligence.

Arcuri, who kept his Buy rating and $185 price target on Nvidia, said completely taking out H20-related revenue would have a $0.20 impact to the company.

In addition, the $5.5B writedown that Nvidia took is “probably” comprised of inventory and the rest in purchase commitments and related reserves.

Assuming $4B in inventory, that would account for roughly $13B in revenue, which Arcuri said is “clearly a significant number.” However, it's unclear how much Nvidia can make up elsewhere, though it is important to note the company did not negatively pre-announce its fiscal first-quarter results and has been adamant that demand is outstripping supply through the rest of 2025.

data.title: Shanghai Electric Group Co., Ltd. reports FY results

• Shanghai Electric Group Co., Ltd. press release (OTC:SIELF): FY revenue of RMB 116.19 billion
  • As of December 31, 2024, total assets stood at 302.51 billion yuan

data.title: Sylvamo CEO Jean-Michel Ribieras to retire, John Sims to succeed
data.description:
• Sylvamo (NYSE:SLVM) has announced that Jean-Michel Ribiéras, chairman and CEO, will retire Dec. 31.
  • John Sims, who currently serves as senior vice president and CFO, has been elected COO by the company’s board of directors, effective May 1.
  • As COO, Sims will lead commercial and operational functions, while Ribiéras continues to lead corporate functions until his retirement. Sims will then become Sylvamo’s next CEO Jan 1, 2026.
  • Don Devlin has been elected senior vice president and CFO to succeed Sims, effective May 1.

data.title: Cullinan gets EU nod to begin testing CLN-978 in rheumatoid arthritis
data.description:
• Cullinan Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CGEM) received European Medicines Agency's approval on Wednesday to initiate trial for CLN-978, the company's rheumatoid arthritis treatment candidate.
  • The Phase 1 trial will test CLN-978, a bispecific CD19 T Cell engager administered subcutaneously, in patients with active, difficult-to-treat rheumatoid arthritis.
  • CLN-978 is also being studied for systemic lupus erythematosus in the U.S., Europe, and Australia, the company said.
  • Stock is up 3.7% premarket.
